title: quercus rehderiana
intro: Quercus rehderiana, also known as Rehder's oak or Chinese white oak, is a deciduous tree native to China and parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the Quercus genus. Its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus rehderiana are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating gastrointestinal issues, and lowering cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be found in the form of dried leaves, bark, and root extracts.
